Alright guys, so the next supplemental visualization exercise I want us to work on is the perfect Testimonial. Now, this is a great efficient way to capture both the moment of highest pleasure as well as the moment of highest tension in a single exercise. So it's a very efficient thing to do. It's a great way to engage your clients in the process or if you don't work with clients anyone else in your organization who is in charge of customer success or just on the front lines of those interactions. So what you want to do is you want to set the stage where it is anywhere between 8 to 12 weeks after someone has joined your program or purchased your service or bought your software. Feel free to play around with that timeline in a way that fits your particular product, but essentially it should be after they've had a chance to enjoy the main benefits of it and have that moment of highest pleasure arise, that moment of redemption. So that's what you want to be looking for on the timeline and they're emailing you because they're so satisfied and totally blown away by the experience that they obviously want to write you this email and thank you for it. So actually do this either pretend to be the customer sending that testimonial, have your client pretend to be that customer or anyone else on your team who is in a great position to do it and it goes like this. Hey, just wanted to reach out with some amazing news yesterday and here's where you'll describe in full detail the main tangible outcome and how it impacted all stakeholders in that person's life, which is pretty awesome, but more than anything, I'm grateful and relieved that this is where you're going to describe that main emotional outcome, usually some sort of relief or redemption over something they didn't think was solvable. And this is just the start. I'm hoping to build upon this initial success and eventually what is that long-term tangible outcome, which I know would really, and of course the emotional benefit that comes with that. Anyway, can't thank you enough for all your training and support. It's pretty hard to believe that only a few months ago I was totally stuck. That moment of highest tension. Thinking that the only way to overcome problem would be to, and here's where you would go into those outdated or effective or inefficient ways that tie into those sacred cows. More than anything, just want to thank you for showing me another way it was possible and delivering on it. A lot of people pay lip service to that, but you and your program really delivered, especially, and this is where you want to really zero in on either a unique feature of it or some kind of mechanism that really differentiates you from your competitors in a powerful and impactful way. All my best, sign off. PS, you probably hear this all the time, but and then what your program is best known for or what you're working towards it being best known for, which is a lot more than your biggest competitor can say winky face because it's always fun to see that. So that's the perfect testimony. You'll feel free to play around with that in ways that best match either your client or your particular product, but that's the basic framework and should really get you a lot of unique insights in a pretty compact, easy to do exercise. So do that one and I'll see you over in exercise three.
